display_prompt:
### SYSTEM
You are an expert data engineer specializing in converting data to text. You will be given the current Python program with its BLUE score and an inspiration program and your task will be to evolve the current Python program to improve the score. It is a program that converts a list of triples into natural language text. The given triples will be associated with the topic: Airport. The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. Converting all those triples into one sentence will award greater score. An example of a complex sentence:
Triples:
(Antwerp | cityServed | Antwerp International Airport)
(Belgium | country | Antwerp)
(City of Brussels | capital | Belgium)
Example sentence:
"Antwerp International Airport serves the city of Antwerp which is in Belgium, where the capital is Brussels."

The 'predict' function returns that sentence as a string. Below is the list of all possible predicates that can be given as an input. With the given predicates and example triples implement the 'predict' function so it can process all the possible predicates:

Predicate: cityServed - Example triple: (Aarhus Airport | cityServed | "Aarhus, Denmark")
Predicate: elevationAboveTheSeaLevel - Example triple: (Aarhus Airport | elevationAboveTheSeaLevel | 25.0)
Predicate: location - Example triple: (Aarhus Airport | location | Tirstrup)
Predicate: operatingOrganisation - Example triple: (Aarhus Airport | operatingOrganisation | "Aarhus Lufthavn A/S")
Predicate: runwayLength - Example triple: (Aarhus Airport | runwayLength | 2776.0)
Predicate: runwayName - Example triple: (Aarhus Airport | runwayName | "10L/28R")
Predicate: country - Example triple: (Abilene, Texas | country | United States)
Predicate: isPartOf - Example triple: (Abilene, Texas | isPartOf | Jones County, Texas)
Predicate: 1stRunwayLengthFeet - Example triple: (Abilene Regional Airport | 1stRunwayLengthFeet | 3678)
Predicate: 1stRunwaySurfaceType - Example triple: (Abilene Regional Airport | 1stRunwaySurfaceType | Asphalt)
Predicate: 3rdRunwayLengthFeet - Example triple: (Abilene Regional Airport | 3rdRunwayLengthFeet | 7202)
Predicate: icaoLocationIdentifier - Example triple: (Abilene Regional Airport | icaoLocationIdentifier | "KABI")
Predicate: locationIdentifier - Example triple: (Abilene Regional Airport | locationIdentifier | "ABI")
Predicate: elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet - Example triple: (Afonso Pena International Airport | elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet | 2988)
Predicate: iataLocationIdentifier - Example triple: (Agra Airport | iataLocationIdentifier | "AGR")
Predicate: nativeName - Example triple: (Agra Airport | nativeName | "Kheria Air Force Station")
Predicate: leaderParty - Example triple: (Alcobendas | leaderParty | People's Party (Spain))
Predicate: capital - Example triple: (Alderney | capital | Saint Anne, Alderney)
Predicate: language - Example triple: (Alderney | language | English language)
Predicate: leader - Example triple: (Alderney | leader | Elizabeth II)
Predicate: owner - Example triple: (Alpena County Regional Airport | owner | Alpena County, Michigan)
Predicate: 1stRunwayLengthMetre - Example triple: (Amsterdam Airport Schiphol | 1stRunwayLengthMetre | 3800)
Predicate: 4thRunwaySurfaceType - Example triple: (Amsterdam Airport Schiphol | 4thRunwaySurfaceType | "Asphalt")
Predicate: 5thRunwayNumber - Example triple: (Amsterdam Airport Schiphol | 5thRunwayNumber | 18)
Predicate: largestCity - Example triple: (Andrews County, Texas | largestCity | Andrews, Texas)
Predicate: 4thRunwayLengthFeet - Example triple: (Andrews County Airport | 4thRunwayLengthFeet | 25)
Predicate: 1stRunwayNumber - Example triple: (Angola International Airport | 1stRunwayNumber | 5)
Predicate: elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres - Example triple: (Angola International Airport | elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres | 159)
Predicate: administrativeArrondissement - Example triple: (Antwerp | administrativeArrondissement | Arrondissement of Antwerp)
Predicate: mayor - Example triple: (Antwerp | mayor | Bart De Wever)
Predicate: 2ndRunwaySurfaceType - Example triple: (Ardmore Airport (New Zealand) | 2ndRunwaySurfaceType | Poaceae)
Predicate: 3rdRunwaySurfaceType - Example triple: (Ardmore Airport (New Zealand) | 3rdRunwaySurfaceType | Poaceae)
Predicate: runwaySurfaceType - Example triple: (Atlantic City International Airport | runwaySurfaceType | "Asphalt/Concrete")

```

The generated text was:
```

```
The example correct sentences are:
The surface of the 1st runway at Alderney Airport is covered with poaceae, which belongs to the division of flowering plants.
The 1st runway at Alderney Airport is made from Poaceae which belongs to the division of flowering plants.
The surface of the 1st runway at Alderney airport is poaceae which is a type of flowering plant.

```

### LOW BLUE SCORE 2
```
The program did very poorly with the given triples, getting BLEU score 0.0. The input triples were:
Alderney Airport | 1stRunwaySurfaceType | Poaceae
Poaceae | order | Poales
Poaceae | order | Commelinids
Poaceae | class | Monocotyledon

The generated text was:
Alderney Airport has 1st runway surface type Poaceae; Poaceae belongs to order Poales, belongs to order Commelinids, and belongs to class Monocotyledon.

The example correct sentences are:
The 1st runway at Alderney Airport is made from Poaceae. Poaceae is of the order Poales and belongs to the order of Commelinids. As well as being part of the class Monocotyledon.
The surface of the 1st runway at Alderney airport is made of poaceae in the order of Poales and Commelinids and in the class of Monocotyledon.

```

# Current Program
```python
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    import re

    if not triples:
        return ""

    def clean(text: str) -> str:
        s = str(text).strip()
        if len(s) >= 2 and s[0] == '"' and s[-1] == '"':
            return s[1:-1]
        return s

    def join_parts(parts: list[str]) -> str:
        if not parts:
            return ""
        if len(parts) == 1:
            return parts[0]
        if len(parts) == 2:
            return f"{parts[0]} and {parts[1]}"
        return f"{', '.join(parts[:-1])}, and {parts[-1]}"

    def render(pred: str, obj: str) -> str:
        o = clean(obj)

        direct = {
            "cityServed": f"serves {o}",
            "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": f"is {o} metres above sea level",
            "location": f"is located in {o}",
            "operatingOrganisation": f"is operated by {o}",
            "runwayLength": f"has a runway length of {o} metres",
            "runwayName": f"has runway named {o}",
            "country": f"is in {o}",
            "isPartOf": f"is part of {o}",
            "icaoLocationIdentifier": f"has ICAO location identifier {o}",
            "locationIdentifier": f"has location identifier {o}",
            "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": f"is {o} feet above sea level",
            "iataLocationIdentifier": f"has IATA location identifier {o}",
            "nativeName": f"is also known as {o}",
            "leaderParty": f"has leader party {o}",
            "capital": f"has capital {o}",
            "language": f"uses {o}",
            "leader": f"is led by {o}",
            "owner": f"is owned by {o}",
            "largestCity": f"has largest city {o}",
            "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": f"is {o} metres above sea level",
            "administrativeArrondissement": f"is in the arrondissement of {o}",
            "mayor": f"has mayor {o}",
            "runwaySurfaceType": f"has runway surface type {o}",
            "officialLanguage": f"has official language {o}",
            "city": f"is in the city of {o}",
            "jurisdiction": f"has jurisdiction over {o}",
            "demonym": f"has demonym {o}",
            "aircraftHelicopter": f"uses helicopter {o}",
            "transportAircraft": f"uses transport aircraft {o}",
            "currency": f"uses currency {o}",
            "headquarter": f"is headquartered at {o}",
            "class": f"belongs to class {o}",
            "division": f"belongs to division {o}",
            "order": f"belongs to order {o}",
            "regionServed": f"serves the region of {o}",
            "leaderTitle": f"has leader title {o}",
            "hubAirport": f"has hub airport {o}",
            "aircraftFighter": f"uses fighter aircraft {o}",
            "attackAircraft": f"uses attack aircraft {o}",
            "battle": f"fought in {o}",
            "countySeat": f"has county seat {o}",
            "chief": f"has chief {o}",
            "foundedBy": f"was founded by {o}",
            "postalCode": f"has postal code {o}",
            "areaCode": f"has area code {o}",
            "foundingYear": f"was founded in {o}",
            "ceremonialCounty": f"is in the ceremonial county of {o}",
        }
        if pred in direct:
            return direct[pred]

        m = re.fullmatch(r"(\d+(?:st|nd|rd|th))Runway(LengthFeet|LengthMetre|Number|SurfaceType)", pred)
        if m:
            n, kind = m.groups()
            if kind == "LengthFeet":
                return f"has {n} runway length of {o} feet"
            if kind == "LengthMetre":
                return f"has {n} runway length of {o} metres"
            if kind == "Number":
                return f"has {n} runway number {o}"
            if kind == "SurfaceType":
                return f"has {n} runway surface type {o}"

        label = re.sub(r"([a-z])([A-Z])", r"\1 \2", pred).lower()
        return f"has {label} {o}"

    grouped: dict[str, list[tuple[str, str]]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        grouped.setdefault(t.subject, []).append((t.predicate, t.object))

    clauses = []
    for subject, facts in grouped.items():
        fragments = [render(p, o) for p, o in facts]
        clauses.append(f"{subject} {join_parts(fragments)}")

    return f"{'; '.join(clauses)}."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
```

## Inspiration Programs

These programs represent diverse approaches and creative solutions that may inspire new ideas:

### Inspiration 1 (Score: 0.2037)
```python
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    return f"The {triples[0].predicate} of {triples[0].subject} is {triples[0].object}."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
```

# Task
Suggest an improvement to the current program that will improve the BLUE Score.
Different solutions with similar BLUE score but different ideas are valuable.
Creating one complex sentence from all the given triples will award greater score as well.

You MUST use the exact SEARCH/REPLACE diff format shown below to indicate changes:

<<<<<<< SEARCH
# Original code to find and replace (must match exactly, indentations and endlines as well)
=======
# New replacement code
>>>>>>> REPLACE

Example of a valid diff format:
<<<<<<< SEARCH
    best_x = 0
    best_y = 0
=======
    # Initialize with a random point
    best_x = np.random.uniform(bounds[0], bounds[1])
    best_y = np.random.uniform(bounds[0], bounds[1])
>>>>>>> REPLACE

The SEARCH section must exactly match the code in the current program, even with it's indentations and endlines.
Give just one SEARCH/REPLACE section.

```python
                return f"has {n} runway number {o}"
            if kind == "SurfaceType":
                return f"has {n} runway surface type {o}"

        label = re.sub(r"([a-z])([A-Z])", r"\1 \2", pred).lower()
        return f"has {label} {o}"

    grouped: dict[str, list[tuple[str, str]]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        grouped.setdefault(t.subject, []).append((t.predicate, t.object))

    clauses = []
    for subject, facts in grouped.items():
        fragments = [render(p, o) for p, o in facts]
        clauses.append(f"{subject} {join_parts(fragments)}")

    return f"{'; '.join(clauses)}."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
```
